test: Phase Coherence — novel room quality metric
description: Measures whether room oscillation reveals keyword structure via Spearman ranking correlation across 4 phase shifts (0, π/2, π, 3π/2)
model: Tiles have 4D keyword vectors. Resolution phase determines which keyword is weighted most heavily. Different phases surface different tile types.
key_insight: Coherence > 0.5 = oscillation reveals structure = room has fractal self-similarity. Coherence < 0.5 = static ranking regardless of phase = room is noise.
springboard_chain: Step 1 (DONE): coherence measures keyword structure via phase-dependent ranking
Step 2: Run on PLATO rooms — compare coherence to tile resolution rate
Step 3: Track coherence over room age — does self-organization increase coherence?
Step 4: Connect to Belyaev: high coherence = diverse keyword dimension span = novel function potential
next_test: Query PLATO rooms (fleet-coord, flux-engine) for tile keywords, project into 4D space, compute coherence
production_implication: Rooms with coherence > 0.5 have fractal phase-dependent structure — they self-organize. Rooms with coherence < 0.5 are static — agents adding tiles creates noise, not structure. This is the QUALITY distinction Casey asked for: not how fast, but whether the room has structure that enables coordination.
